Title: Gene discovery and virus-induced gene silencing reveal branched pathways to major classes of bioactive diterpenoids in Euphorbia peplus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots and stems; intermediate in developing pods; low in latex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots and stems; intermediate in developing pods; low in latex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex and latex-containing tissues (main stems, side stems, leaves); ingenanes also in dry seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive diterpenoids including medicinal ingenanes and multidrug-resistance-reversing jatrophanes
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream common pathway to jolkinol C, shared by jatrophane and ingenane branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to the macrocyclic diterpene casbene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for both target-product branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control step for pathway flux to both jatrophanes and ingenanes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing in Euphorbia peplus; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing decreased both jatrophane and ingenane levels by over twofold in stems and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpCAS is required for production of the two major E. peplus diterpenoid classes, jatrophanes and ingenanes. 2. VIGS of EpCAS reduced EpCAS transcript levels about threefold to fourfold in stems. 3. Jatrophanes and ingenanes were both reduced, supporting casbene as the common precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effect on peplusol levels
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point gene for reconstructing or suppressing casbene-derived diterpenoid flux; should be combined with downstream branch genes to direct product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in 9-d-old seedlings; metabolites analyzed in chlorotic stems and leaves around 40 d postinfiltration
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GenBank BioProject PRJNA81996; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene is physically linked to CYP726A19 in a diterpenoid gene cluster; VIGS supports long-held hypothesis that jatrophanes and ingenanes are casbene-derived

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in stems and pods; lower in latex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in stems and pods; lower in latex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex and latex-containing tissues; ingenanes also in dry seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive diterpenoids including medicinal ingenanes and multidrug-resistance-reversing jatrophanes
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Casbene-derived diterpenoid biosynthesis to jolkinol C
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared jolkinol C-forming pathway before branch to jatrophanes and ingenanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes casbene-derived intermediates at C9 during conversion of casbene toward jolkinol C
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream shared step upstream of branch point
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor required for both target-product branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for jolkinol C formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing in Euphorbia peplus; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; prior heterologous expression evidence referenced in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced both jatrophane and ingenane levels by over twofold in stems and caused accumulation of 5-ketocasbene and 6-hydroxy-5-ketocasbene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71D365 is required for efficient synthesis of both jatrophanes and ingenanes. 2. VIGS reduced CYP71D365 transcript levels about threefold to fourfold and lowered both product classes. 3. Accumulation of upstream casbene oxidation intermediates indicates CYP71D365 acts in jolkinol C biosynthesis and supports a sequence in which C5/C6 oxidation precedes C9 oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Peplusol levels were also reduced in stems, possibly due to off-target or additional function not resolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important shared-pathway gene for engineering jolkinol C supply to downstream diterpenoid branches; caution that silencing affected peplusol
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in 9-d-old seedlings; metabolites analyzed in chlorotic stems and leaves around 40 d postinfiltration
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GenBank BioProject PRJNA81996; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific VIGS showed no reduction in transcript levels of other CYP71D homologs; located in gene cluster 2

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in latex; also detected in stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in latex; also detected in stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jatrophanes are bioactive diterpenoids, including multidrug-resistance reversers
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Jatrophane branch after jolkinol C branch point
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the C3 keto group of jolkinol C to form jolkinol E; also reduces epi-jolkinol C to epi-jolkinol E in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: First committed step in jatrophane branch after jolkinol C
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms branch-specific intermediate leading to jatrophanes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme; first step from jolkinol C to jatrophane production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; NMR structural elucidation; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; VIGS in Euphorbia peplus; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jatrophane production; silencing lowered jatrophanes and redirected flux to increase ingenanes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpSDR-5 converts jolkinol C to jolkinol E and thus catalyzes the first identified branch-specific step toward jatrophanes. 2. Function was confirmed in both N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae, with NMR verification of jolkinol E. 3. VIGS of EpSDR-5 decreased jatrophanes and increased ingenanes, demonstrating pathway branching at jolkinol C.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing redirected flux toward ingenanes; no effect on peplusol reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering to bias flux between jatrophane and ingenane products; downregulation could enhance ingenane/ingenol mebutate-related accumulation while overexpression may favor jatrophanes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; VIGS in Euphorbia peplus seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector in Nicotiana benthamiana; pTRV2-based VIGS in Euphorbia peplus
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GenBank BioProject PRJNA81996; LC-MS metabolomics; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not located in either diterpenoid gene cluster; likely native role is jolkinol C reduction, while epi-jolkinol C reduction may be promiscuous

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex and/or stems
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Casbene-derived diterpenoid side reaction in heterologous assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Unclear; likely side-route from noncyclized casbene oxidation product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces a proposed casbene-derived dione intermediate to a dihydroxy ketone in Nicotiana benthamiana assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Not established as connected to major target end products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS; NMR; negative test in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced a reduced casbene-derived product in N. benthamiana but no evidence it contributes to major E. peplus jatrophane or ingenane biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpSDR-1 generated a new metabolite identified as (3E,6E,11E)-8,9-dihydroxy-casba-3,6,11-trien-5-one in N. benthamiana. 2. Activity was not reproduced in S. cerevisiae because the proposed substrate did not accumulate there. 3. The paper does not support EpSDR-1 as a core in vivo gene for the major jatrophane/ingenane pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited due to uncertain relevance to native target diterpenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GenBank BioProject PRJNA81996; LC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally characterized in Results, but likely not a core causal gene for the major nutrient/metabolite branches in planta
